reviews.review_msg: Diana is amazing with teaching this course, she explains everything perfectly. I think the bad ratings are coming from the arts/social sciences students who are taking this as a science breadth course and I wouldn't recommend that because you actually need knowledge about the basics. This was probably one of the easier 100 level science courses.
Get ready to teach yourself apparently you have to teach this course to yourself with no help and zero idea of the concepts.
. Wayyyyyyyy too stressful for intro class. And u have to pay for HER textbook... Do not take

She's very friendly and you can tell she's passionate about what she teaches but her exams are pretty difficult. 80% of it is fair but there will be a couple questions that make you mad because she's purposefully trying to trick you. She often makes announcements at the last minute which is annoying as some of us take an hour to get to class.

She is so sweet and clearly wants to help you learn more about your body and improve your relationship with food. You have to buy her textbook and she teaches using a flipped classroom model. Her exams were hard but fair, and there were biweekly quizzes in tutorial. Make sure you stick on top of readings because there is a lot of material

assignments are easy and worth a good portion of your grade however fina and midterm are tough and you need to memerize all processes in textbook. prof was super nice tho and made lectures/online videos intresting. mc quizzes in tutorial every two weeks were hard but not worth much. i would take this course if you need a science/bpk.

Diana had very engaging lectures, and the concepts of the class were interesting and fun to learn. Unfortunately, the tests did not at all encompass what we learned in class. She would test on minor details found in the textbook that were not covered in lecture or mentioned at all throughout the course. She constantly changed deadlines as well.

Diana is super caring and a great lecturer. Fair exams and quizzes. However, the projects dented my grade, which is disappointing considering how much work/time I put into them. By part 3 of the assignment, I was super stressed and gave up. My grade was in the A range before the project and now I'm at B+...so my efforts feel a bit wasted.
Engaging lectures, but exams focus on minor details. Though textbook is required, unstuck study can be a helpful study tool alongside it. Overall, manageable with consistent effort.

Honestly her lecture videos are great and engaging, but then you get to the exam and it has not a SINGLE thing from the lecture, super frustrating. She asks the smallest details on the exams which is stupid. Assignments were easy but tbh don't take this class unless you need to.

Her online lectures were done well and she seems very nice. Her projects were interesting and well graded however, midterm asks lots of minor detailed questions and you need to read the textbook for them.

Pro: Teaches the material well and is passionate
Con: Exams focus on minor details. Exams are short answers, fill-in-the-blanks, and multiple choice. If you wish to get a high grade, you must read the textbook thoroughly. The questions in the exams require the ability to apply material, not definitions. Exams were unnecessarily stressful.

She is a queen, she taught 326 very well in my opinion. I recommend taking 326 with her if you get the chance, it is not an easy class but she does a pretty good job at teaching it. She is also very raw and real and you can tell she is constantly trying to improve and help us in any way she can. Great teacher overall

I've taken several of Diana's classes and have enjoyed them. I particularly enjoyed her teaching 340. She does have a voice that puts you to sleep at 8:30a, but she's good at breaking lectures up into group activities that are related to the final. Can be a difficult grader, exams based on lectures and readings. Really well presented OL courses.
3 projects, midterm, final + biweekly quizzes. Easy course. Okay prof. Diana wasn't bad, but wasn't very accessible. Makes us buy her own textbook but its only $25 and you don't reallllly need it.
